title: Can I help you, madam?
scene: 一位顾客走进服装店挑选衣物，售货员上前询问需要什么、想要什么颜色和样式，两人围绕购物需求做简短交流。重点在 want 的用法与 some/any。

words.cn: 夫人；女士（对女顾客的尊称）
words.eg: Can I help you, madam? 夫人，我能为您效劳吗？
I want a new coat. 我想要一件新外套。
What size do you want? 你要多大尺码？
I like this colour. 我喜欢这个颜色。
This material feels soft. 这种布料摸起来很软。
That is a pretty dress. 那是一条漂亮的连衣裙。
The butcher has some fresh meat today.
He did not finish his egg, but he laughed happily.
She needs some sugar and a pound of butter.
Not a penny was missing; the dream had been pure invention.
Honey flowed out when the wood was cut. 木板切开时蜂蜜流了出来。
Ripe fruit falls from the trees and you never have to work.
it can become a 'flying fruit-bowl', carrying bananas from the plantations to the ports;
We haven't got much tea or coffee, and we haven't got any sugar or jam.
Yes, and I am going to make a sweet dessert.
she had to wear short socks and a bright, orange-coloured dress.
you have the choice of very expensive organically-grown vegetables
This was followed, at the end of the 1970s, by a machine called an Apple.

grammar.point: want + 名词 / want to do
grammar.explain: want 表示“想要”。后接名词表示想要某物；后接 to + 动词原形表示想做某事。
examples: I want some blue material.（我想要一些蓝色的布料。）
She wants to buy a hat.（她想买一顶帽子。）

grammar.point: some 与 any 的基本区别
grammar.explain: some 常用于肯定句，any 常用于否定句和一般疑问句，都表示“一些”。
examples: I want some green material.（我想要一些绿色的布料。）
Do you want any buttons?（你要一些纽扣吗？）

tips: ①对女顾客礼貌称呼用 madam，对男顾客用 sir。②want 想要具体某物时后接名词：I want a dress；想做某事时用 want to do。③购物时问尺码用 What size…?，问颜色用 What colour…?。④肯定句里表“一些”多用 some，疑问和否定里多用 any。

title: Is that all?
scene: 一位顾客在食品杂货店里逐样报出要买的东西，店员一边记一边确认还要不要别的。对话围绕食品数量与包装量词，重点是 some/any 和可数不可数名词。

words.eg: I want a tin of fish. 我想要一罐鱼罐头。
We need a loaf of bread. 我们需要一条面包。
She bought a bottle of milk. 她买了一瓶牛奶。
I want a pound of sugar. 我想要一磅糖。
There is some flour in the bag. 袋子里有一些面粉。
Do you want any cheese? 你要一些奶酪吗？
Have we got any beer and wine?
Have we got any beer and wine?
Hello. Were you at the butcher's?
Do you want any meat today, Mrs. Bird?
Do you want beef or lamb?
Do you want beef or lamb?
Her husband is cleaning the house and buying some drinks.
What about some steak?
And a pound of mince, too.
Do you want a chicken, Mrs. Bird?
To tell you the truth, Mrs. Bird,

grammar.point: 可数名词与不可数名词
grammar.explain: 可数名词能直接用数字和复数计数，如 two tins；不可数名词（如 flour、cheese、milk）不能直接数，要用量词短语，如 a pound of flour、a bottle of milk。
examples: I want two loaves of bread.（我想要两条面包。）
She wants some cheese and a bottle of milk.（她想要一些奶酪和一瓶牛奶。）

grammar.point: some 与 any 的选用
grammar.explain: 肯定陈述句用 some；否定句和一般疑问句一般用 any。二者都可修饰可数名词复数或不可数名词。
examples: There is some sugar in the tin.（罐子里有一些糖。）
We haven't got any flour.（我们没有面粉了。）

tips: ①不可数名词计量要靠量词：a tin of… / a loaf of… / a bottle of… / a pound of…。②loaf 的复数是 loaves，要注意变化。③Is that all? 意为“就这些吗？”，是店员常用的确认语。④肯定句用 some，否定和疑问句多用 any。

title: A bad cold
scene: 有人打电话找朋友，接电话的家人说他病得不轻正卧床休息，不能来接电话，还叮嘱他必须待在床上。对话围绕生病的状态，重点是现在进行时和情态动词 must。

words.eg: He has a bad cold today. 他今天得了重感冒。
I have a cold and I feel tired. 我感冒了，觉得很累。
What's the matter with you? 你怎么了？
He has a high temperature. 他发烧了。
You must stay in bed today. 你今天必须待在床上。
You need a lot of rest. 你需要好好休息。
To tell you the truth, Mrs. Bird,
I don't like chicken either!
We haven't got many tomatoes, but we've got a lot of potatoes.
We need some potatoes for dinner. 晚饭我们需要一些土豆。
Are you going to buy a cabbage?
There are some beans on the plate. 盘子里有一些豆子。
And so with solids such as a shell, a nut, a plum, a pear, a tadpole, a mushroom,
Was the writer successful in protecting his peach tree? Why not?
I come from Greece.
An interesting climate

grammar.point: 现在进行时描述当下的状态或动作
grammar.explain: 现在进行时由 be + 动词-ing 构成，表示此刻正在发生的动作或当前的情形。
examples: He is staying in bed now.（他现在正卧床休息。）
I am not feeling well today.（我今天感觉不太舒服。）

grammar.point: 情态动词 must 表示必须
grammar.explain: must 后接动词原形，表示“必须、一定要”，语气较强。否定 mustn't 表示“禁止、不许”。
examples: You must see a doctor.（你必须去看医生。）
You mustn't get up now.（你现在不许起床。）

tips: ①问别人怎么了用 What's the matter (with you)?。②have a bad cold 表示得了重感冒，have a high temperature 表示发烧。③must 后跟动词原形，且各人称形式不变。④mustn't 表示“禁止”，不要与 needn't（不必）混淆。

title: Thank you, doctor.
scene: 医生上门复诊，家人报告病人的情况比昨天好了一些，医生检查后给出建议，家属道谢送别。对话围绕病情好转，重点是形容词比较级和现在进行时。

words.eg: The doctor is coming this afternoon. 医生今天下午会来。
He is much better today. 他今天好多了。
She is quite well now. 她现在完全好了。
He is still in bed. 他还躺在床上。
Take this medicine twice a day. 这药一天吃两次。
He will get well soon. 他很快就会好起来。
We're going to spend three days in the country!
It's mild, but it's not always pleasant.
The weather's often cold in the North and windy in the East.
I like spring and summer.
The weather's often cold in the North and windy in the East.
He can get up for about two hours each day, but you must keep the room warm.
Does it often rain in summer?
Mr. Sawyer usually reads his newspaper, but sometimes he and his wife watch television.
I like spring and summer.
I don't like autumn and winter.
I don't like autumn and winter.
Does it snow here in winter? 这儿冬天下雪吗？

grammar.point: 形容词比较级（含不规则 good→better）
grammar.explain: 比较两者时用比较级，常在形容词后加 -er，多音节词用 more。good 和 well 的比较级都是不规则的 better，表示“更好、好一些”。
examples: He feels better than yesterday.（他感觉比昨天好些了。）
This medicine is stronger than that one.（这种药比那种药更强效。）

grammar.point: 现在进行时表示正在进行的动作
grammar.explain: be + 动词-ing 表示说话时正在发生的动作。
examples: The doctor is checking the patient.（医生正在检查病人。）
He is taking his medicine now.（他正在吃药。）

tips: ①better 是 good/well 的比较级，问病情常说 Is he better today?。②比较级句型常配 than：better than yesterday。③still 表示“仍然”，常放在实义动词前、be 动词后。④get well 意为“康复、好起来”。

title: Not a baby
scene: 一个孩子想晚点睡、想自己做主，家长强调该睡觉了必须听话；孩子据理力争说自己已经长大不是小宝宝了。对话围绕请求与命令，重点是情态动词 can 和 must。

words.eg: I am not a baby now. 我现在不是小孩子了。
It is late. You must go to bed. 天晚了，你必须睡觉了。
You must go to bed now. 你现在必须上床睡觉。
Can I stay up tonight? 我今晚可以晚点睡吗？
He talks like a grown-up. 他说话像个大人。
You must listen to me. 你必须听我的。
It's often cold in December, January and February.
It's often cold in December, January and February.
It's often windy in March.
Can you come at 10 a.m. on Monday, April 24th?
It's always warm in April and May, but it rains sometimes.
It's always hot in June, July and August.
It's always hot in June, July and August.
It's always hot in June, July and August.
It's always warm in September and October.
It's always warm in September and October.
It's often cold in November and it rains sometimes.
It's often cold in December, January and February.
At the base of the gold cap, the words 'made in the U.S.A.' had been neatly inscribed.

grammar.point: 情态动词 can 表示能力与许可
grammar.explain: can 后接动词原形，可表示“能够”，也可用于请求或给予许可（Can I…? / You can…）。否定为 can't。
examples: Can I stay up a little longer?（我可以再晚睡一会儿吗？）
You can read one more page.（你可以再读一页。）

grammar.point: 情态动词 must 表示必须
grammar.explain: must 后接动词原形，表示“必须、一定要”，各人称形式相同。否定 mustn't 表示“不许”。
examples: You must go to bed now.（你现在必须上床睡觉。）
You mustn't stay up so late.（你不许熬这么晚。）

tips: ①Can I…? 是请求许可的常用句型，回答用 Yes, you can / No, you can't。②must 表示说话人认为“必须”，语气强。③stay up 意为“熬夜、不去睡”。④can 和 must 后面都直接跟动词原形，不加 to。

title: The weekend
scene: 两人聊起周末的安排，一方问对方这个周末打算做什么，另一方说家里要来客人正忙着准备。对话围绕近期计划，重点是用现在进行时表示已安排好的将来活动。

words.eg: What are you doing this weekend? 这个周末你打算做什么？
We are expecting some friends tonight. 今晚我们在等几位朋友来。
Our guests are coming on Saturday. 我们的客人星期六来。
She is preparing dinner now. 她正在准备晚饭。
I am very busy this weekend. 这个周末我很忙。
We are cooking a big meal for them. 我们正在为他们做一顿丰盛的饭。
The flap of a butterfly's wings in Brazil might, in theory, set off a tornado in Texas.
My daughter, Jane, never dreamed of receiving a letter from a girl of her own age in Holland.
I come from England.
After I had left a small village in the south of France,
It was made in Germany in 1681.
Last summer, I went to Italy.
The Karen had been sailing in a convoy to Russia when she was torpedoed by an enemy submarine.
He flew to Spain a week ago.
During a holiday in Sweden,
It's mild, but it's not always pleasant.
She always comes home early.

grammar.point: 现在进行时表示已安排好的将来计划
grammar.explain: be + 动词-ing 除了表示正在发生的动作，也常和表将来的时间状语（如 this weekend、tonight）连用，表示已经安排好、即将进行的活动。
examples: We are having guests this weekend.（这个周末我们家要来客人。）
She is leaving tomorrow morning.（她明天早上就要走了。）

tips: ①What are you doing this weekend? 问的是周末的安排，不是此刻的动作。②带 this weekend、tonight、tomorrow 等将来时间时，进行时表示计划中的将来。③expect a guest 意为“等候客人到来”。④be busy doing sth 表示“忙于做某事”。

title: The car race
scene: 两人在看一场汽车比赛，讨论哪辆车最快、谁将会取胜，比较各辆赛车的快慢与好坏。对话围绕比较与预测，重点是形容词最高级、比较级以及表将来的表达。

words.eg: The car race is very exciting. 这场赛车非常激动人心。
This car is very fast. 这辆车非常快。
It is the fastest car in the race. 它是这场比赛里最快的车。
The driver in the red car is very good. 红色车里的驾驶员很出色。
Which car will win the race? 哪辆车会赢得比赛？
It is a very exciting race. 这是一场非常激动人心的比赛。
The weather's often cold in the North and windy in the East.
The weather's often cold in the North and windy in the East.
It's often wet in the West and sometimes warm in the South.
It's often wet in the West and sometimes warm in the South.
It's often wet in the West and sometimes warm in the South.
Autumn is my favourite season. 秋天是我最喜欢的季节。
He is the best driver in the team. 他是队里最好的车手。
Did he telephone again last night?
The sun rises early and sets late.
He often starts at ten o'clock and never stops early.
The sun rises early and sets late.
You mustn't come home late.
At the moment, he's reading an interesting book.

grammar.explain: 在三者或三者以上中表示“最……”用最高级，单音节形容词加 -est，前面常加 the，多音节词用 most。fast→fastest。
examples: It is the fastest car in the world.（它是世界上最快的车。）
He is the best driver in the team.（他是队里最好的车手。）

grammar.explain: 比较两者时用比较级，单音节词加 -er，常与 than 连用。fast→faster。
examples: The red car is faster than the blue one.（红色的车比蓝色的快。）
This driver is better than that one.（这位车手比那位更棒。）

grammar.explain: will + 动词原形表示对将来的预测或判断，各人称相同。
examples: Which car will win?（哪辆车会赢？）
I think the red car will be first.（我认为红色的车会得第一。）

tips: ①最高级前一般加 the，后接范围用 in/of：the fastest car in the world。②比较级配 than，最高级配范围短语，别混用。③fast 的比较级 faster、最高级 fastest；good 的是 better/best（不规则）。④预测将来用 will + 动词原形。
